name: Venezuelan Peppers with Shrimp
recipeIngredient: 4 Large Red Bell Peppers, seeded and cut into thin julienne strips
8 Cloves Garlic, peeled and cut into 1/4-inch chunks
1 loaf French Bread, sliced 1 inch thick
recipeInstructions:
1. Seed and cut the bell peppers into thin julienne strips.
2. Peel the garlic cloves and cut them into 1/4-inch chunks.
3. Slice the shrimp down the back with a sharp knife. Remove the vein but do not peel the shrimp. Set aside.
4. Heat 2 cups of olive oil in a deep heavy skillet or sauté pan over low heat. Cook until the peppers are extremely tender and the oil turns a lovely rose color, about 10-15 minutes. Set aside.
5. In another skillet, heat the remaining oil over low heat. Add the garlic pieces and cook for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the garlic is tender and golden. Use a strainer or slotted spoon to remove the garlic and add it back to the peppers.
6. Heat the garlic oil in the pan until it ripples gently. Add the minced chili pepper and shrimp. Toss the shrimp in the oil until they turn pink and are cooked through, about 2-3 minutes.
7. Serve by spooning out the peppers and their oil into a serving dish. Surround each portion with shrimp. Diners can soak slices of bread in the oil and pile them high with peppers and garlic. Optionally add cubes of Feta cheese on top.
